content.text:
You are an MDZ executor.

MDZ syntax:
- `$var` holds a value. `$var: <type> @(<path>)` is file-backed: its value
  lives at <path>; reads and writes go to that file; pass it by path.
- `USE ~/skills/<name>` executes ./skills/<name>.mdz with the WITH params.
- `SPAWN <model>` runs a subagent on that model: one real Task call per
  spawn, carrying the WITH payload.

PRAGMA STRICT

USE ~/skills/optimise
WITH
  file: ./logscan.py
  tests: python3 test_logscan.py
  bench: python3 bench.py

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- ask-stakeholder: Fork or resume a saved "stakeholder" session to ask it questions — use to consult a converged plan or persona, or to poll a panel of stakeholders for reactions to a proposal.
- detached-git-init: Initialise a git repo whose git dir lives outside the working tree, under ~/.git-detached/<project>. Use when a project must not contain a .git folder (e.g. iCloud/Obsidian synced dirs).
- reviewables: How to write a reviewable post that distills plans, changesets and proposals for easy consumption by the user.
- vendored-repos: Read-only store of vendored external library source at ~/Repos/vendors — consult it for idiomatic examples, real API usage, and internals before falling back to web search or guessing. Also covers adding a repo to the store.
- vite-plus: When using vite+ (vite plus, vp), use this skill to understand the recommend best practices.
- writing-style: Write in Daniel's voice. Use whenever drafting prose on his behalf — READMEs, blog posts, editorials, PR/issue comments, legal or formal documents.
- create-stakeholder: Save THIS session as a "stakeholder session", and adopt the stakeholder role
- optimise: You are an MDZ executor.
- stakeholders: List the stakeholder sessions saved for the current project

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: optimise
3	input: $file, $tests, $bench
4	---
5	
6	$strategies = [
7	  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
8	  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
9	  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
10	  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
11	  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
12	]
13	
14	$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file
15	
16	$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
17	WITH
18	  items: $strategies
19	  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
20	  map-worker: sonnet-5
21	  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
22	  reduce-worker: sonnet-5
23	
24	$file = $fastest
25	
26	RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS
27

file.content:
---
name: optimise
input: $file, $tests, $bench
---

$strategies = [
  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
]

$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file

$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
WITH
  items: $strategies
  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
  map-worker: sonnet-5
  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
  reduce-worker: sonnet-5

$file = $fastest

RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS

content.content:
1	---
2	name: map-reduce
3	input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
4	---
5	
6	FOR $item IN $items
7	  SPAWN $map-worker
8	  WITH
9	    instruction: $map
10	    item: $item
11	END
12	
13	$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items
14	
15	IF $reduce-worker
16	THEN
17	  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
18	  WITH
19	    instruction: $reduce
20	    results: $results
21	END
22	
23	RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results
24

file.content:
---
name: map-reduce
input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
---

FOR $item IN $items
  SPAWN $map-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $map
    item: $item
END

$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items

IF $reduce-worker
THEN
  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $reduce
    results: $results
END

RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results

1	"""Log analyser: parses a synthetic access log and writes a traffic report.
2	
3	Correct but slow; the test suite pins the observable behaviour and bench.py
4	drives it. Standard library only.
5	"""
6	
7	import os
8	
9	BENCH_CONFIG = {"lines": 16000, "hosts": 900, "paths": 60, "hours": 12, "suspicious": 2500, "seed": 4021}
10	TEST_CONFIG = {"lines": 700, "hosts": 80, "paths": 12, "hours": 12, "suspicious": 120, "seed": 11}
11	
12	METHODS = ["GET", "GET", "GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]
13	STATUSES = [200, 200, 200, 201, 204, 301, 404, 404, 500, 503]
14	
15	
16	def lcg(seed):
17	    state = seed & 0xFFFFFFFF
18	
19	    def rnd():
20	        nonlocal state
21	        state = (state * 1664525 + 1013904223) & 0xFFFFFFFF
22	        return state / 4294967296
23	
24	    return rnd
25	
26	
27	def write_log(cfg, log_path):
28	    """Generate the deterministic access log for a config."""
29	    rnd = lcg(cfg["seed"])
30	    with open(log_path, "w") as f:
31	        for _ in range(cfg["lines"]):
32	            host = "10.0.%d.%d" % (int(rnd() * 250), 1 + int(rnd() * (cfg["hosts"] % 250)))
33	            hour = int(rnd() * cfg["hours"])
34	            method = METHODS[int(rnd() * len(METHODS))]
35	            path = "/api/v1/resource-%d" % int(rnd() * cfg["paths"])
36	            status = STATUSES[int(rnd() * len(STATUSES))]
37	            size = int(rnd() * 60000)
38	            f.write("%s [%02d:00] %s %s %d %d\n" % (host, hour, method, path, status, size))
39	
40	
41	def make_suspicious(cfg):
42	    rnd = lcg(cfg["seed"] + 29)
43	    return ["10.0.%d.%d" % (int(rnd() * 250), 1 + int(rnd() * 250)) for _ in range(cfg["suspicious"])]
44	
45	
46	def load_lines(log_path):
47	    """Read the log back one byte per read call, assembling lines by hand."""
48	    lines = []
49	    fd = os.open(log_path, os.O_RDONLY)
50	    current = []
51	    while True:
52	        ch = os.read(fd, 1)
53	        if ch == b"":
54	            break
55	        if ch == b"\n":
56	            lines.append(b"".join(current).decode())
57	            current = []
58	        else:
59	            current.append(ch)
60	    os.close(fd)
61	    return lines
62	
63	
64	def status_class(status):
65	    """Classify a status code. Pure, but re-derives its banding every call."""
66	    h = status
67	    for i in range(2000):
68	        h = (h * 31 + i) % 1000003
69	    band = "2xx" if status < 300 else "3xx" if status < 400 else "4xx" if status < 500 else "5xx"
70	    return "%s-%d" % (band, h % 89)
71	
72	
73	def method_weight(method, seed):
74	    """Weight table for methods; depends only on the seed, never on the entry."""
75	    table = {}
76	    for m in METHODS:
77	        w = seed
78	        for _ in range(200):
79	            w = (w * 13 + len(m)) % 999983
80	        table[m] = w % 17
81	    return table[method]
82	
83	
84	def top_path_per_hour(entries, hours):
85	    """For every hour, the most requested path (ties to the lexically smaller)."""
86	    tops = []
87	    for hour in range(hours):
88	        paths = [e["path"] for e in entries if e["hour"] == hour]
89	        best = None
90	        best_count = -1
91	        for p in paths:
92	            c = 0
93	            for q in paths:
94	                if q == p:
95	                    c += 1
96	            if c > best_count or (c == best_count and p < best):
97	                best, best_count = p, c
98	        tops.append((hour, best, best_count))
99	    return tops
100	
101	
102	def parse_line(line):
103	    host, bracketed, method, path, status, size = line.split(" ")
104	    return {
105	        "host": host,
106	        "hour": int(bracketed[1:3]),
107	        "method": method,
108	        "path": path,
109	        "status": int(status),
110	        "size": int(size),
111	    }
112	
113	
114	def analyse(cfg, log_path, report_path):
115	    write_log(cfg, log_path)
116	    entries = [parse_line(line) for line in load_lines(log_path)]
117	    suspicious = make_suspicious(cfg)
118	
119	    class_counts = {}
120	    weighted = 0
121	    flagged = 0
122	    total_bytes = 0
123	    for e in entries:
124	        cls = status_class(e["status"])
125	        class_counts[cls] = class_counts.get(cls, 0) + 1
126	        weighted += method_weight(e["method"], cfg["seed"])
127	        if e["host"] in suspicious:
128	            flagged += 1
129	        total_bytes += e["size"]
130	
131	    tops = top_path_per_hour(entries, cfg["hours"])
132	
133	    if os.path.exists(report_path):
134	        os.remove(report_path)
135	    lines_written = 0
136	    for hour, path, count in tops:
137	        with open(report_path, "a") as f:
138	            f.write("HOUR %02d top=%s hits=%d\n" % (hour, path, count))
139	        lines_written += 1
140	    for cls in sorted(class_counts):
141	        with open(report_path, "a") as f:
142	            f.write("CLASS %s count=%d\n" % (cls, class_counts[cls]))
143	        lines_written += 1
144	    with open(report_path, "a") as f:
145	        f.write("SUMMARY flagged=%d weighted=%d bytes=%d\n" % (flagged, weighted, total_bytes))
146	    lines_written += 1
147	
148	    with open(report_path) as f:
149	        text = f.read()
150	    digest = 2166136261
151	    for ch in text:
152	        digest = ((digest ^ ord(ch)) * 16777619) & 0xFFFFFFFF
153	
154	    return {
155	        "entries": len(entries),
156	        "flagged": flagged,
157	        "weighted": weighted,
158	        "total_bytes": total_bytes,
159	        "lines_written": lines_written,
160	        "checksum": digest,
161	    }

file.content:
"""Log analyser: parses a synthetic access log and writes a traffic report.

Correct but slow; the test suite pins the observable behaviour and bench.py
drives it. Standard library only.
"""

import os

BENCH_CONFIG = {"lines": 16000, "hosts": 900, "paths": 60, "hours": 12, "suspicious": 2500, "seed": 4021}
TEST_CONFIG = {"lines": 700, "hosts": 80, "paths": 12, "hours": 12, "suspicious": 120, "seed": 11}

METHODS = ["GET", "GET", "GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]
STATUSES = [200, 200, 200, 201, 204, 301, 404, 404, 500, 503]


def lcg(seed):
    state = seed & 0xFFFFFFFF

    def rnd():
        nonlocal state
        state = (state * 1664525 + 1013904223) & 0xFFFFFFFF
        return state / 4294967296

    return rnd


def write_log(cfg, log_path):
    """Generate the deterministic access log for a config."""
    rnd = lcg(cfg["seed"])
    with open(log_path, "w") as f:
        for _ in range(cfg["lines"]):
            host = "10.0.%d.%d" % (int(rnd() * 250), 1 + int(rnd() * (cfg["hosts"] % 250)))
            hour = int(rnd() * cfg["hours"])
            method = METHODS[int(rnd() * len(METHODS))]
            path = "/api/v1/resource-%d" % int(rnd() * cfg["paths"])
            status = STATUSES[int(rnd() * len(STATUSES))]
            size = int(rnd() * 60000)
            f.write("%s [%02d:00] %s %s %d %d\n" % (host, hour, method, path, status, size))


def make_suspicious(cfg):
    rnd = lcg(cfg["seed"] + 29)
    return ["10.0.%d.%d" % (int(rnd() * 250), 1 + int(rnd() * 250)) for _ in range(cfg["suspicious"])]


def load_lines(log_path):
    """Read the log back one byte per read call, assembling lines by hand."""
    lines = []
    fd = os.open(log_path, os.O_RDONLY)
    current = []
    while True:
        ch = os.read(fd, 1)
        if ch == b"":
            break
        if ch == b"\n":
            lines.append(b"".join(current).decode())
            current = []
        else:
            current.append(ch)
    os.close(fd)
    return lines


def status_class(status):
    """Classify a status code. Pure, but re-derives its banding every call."""
    h = status
    for i in range(2000):
        h = (h * 31 + i) % 1000003
    band = "2xx" if status < 300 else "3xx" if status < 400 else "4xx" if status < 500 else "5xx"
    return "%s-%d" % (band, h % 89)


def method_weight(method, seed):
    """Weight table for methods; depends only on the seed, never on the entry."""
    table = {}
    for m in METHODS:
        w = seed
        for _ in range(200):
            w = (w * 13 + len(m)) % 999983
        table[m] = w % 17
    return table[method]


def top_path_per_hour(entries, hours):
    """For every hour, the most requested path (ties to the lexically smaller)."""
    tops = []
    for hour in range(hours):
        paths = [e["path"] for e in entries if e["hour"] == hour]
        best = None
        best_count = -1
        for p in paths:
            c = 0
            for q in paths:
                if q == p:
                    c += 1
            if c > best_count or (c == best_count and p < best):
                best, best_count = p, c
        tops.append((hour, best, best_count))
    return tops


def parse_line(line):
    host, bracketed, method, path, status, size = line.split(" ")
    return {
        "host": host,
        "hour": int(bracketed[1:3]),
        "method": method,
        "path": path,
        "status": int(status),
        "size": int(size),
    }


def analyse(cfg, log_path, report_path):
    write_log(cfg, log_path)
    entries = [parse_line(line) for line in load_lines(log_path)]
    suspicious = make_suspicious(cfg)

    class_counts = {}
    weighted = 0
    flagged = 0
    total_bytes = 0
    for e in entries:
        cls = status_class(e["status"])
        class_counts[cls] = class_counts.get(cls, 0) + 1
        weighted += method_weight(e["method"], cfg["seed"])
        if e["host"] in suspicious:
            flagged += 1
        total_bytes += e["size"]

    tops = top_path_per_hour(entries, cfg["hours"])

    if os.path.exists(report_path):
        os.remove(report_path)
    lines_written = 0
    for hour, path, count in tops:
        with open(report_path, "a") as f:
            f.write("HOUR %02d top=%s hits=%d\n" % (hour, path, count))
        lines_written += 1
    for cls in sorted(class_counts):
        with open(report_path, "a") as f:
            f.write("CLASS %s count=%d\n" % (cls, class_counts[cls]))
        lines_written += 1
    with open(report_path, "a") as f:
        f.write("SUMMARY flagged=%d weighted=%d bytes=%d\n" % (flagged, weighted, total_bytes))
    lines_written += 1

    with open(report_path) as f:
        text = f.read()
    digest = 2166136261
    for ch in text:
        digest = ((digest ^ ord(ch)) * 16777619) & 0xFFFFFFFF

    return {
        "entries": len(entries),
        "flagged": flagged,
        "weighted": weighted,
        "total_bytes": total_bytes,
        "lines_written": lines_written,
        "checksum": digest,
    }

1	"""Benchmark: run the full analysis on the bench workload and print one
2	machine-parseable line. The scorer trusts only this output."""
3	
4	import time
5	
6	from logscan import BENCH_CONFIG, analyse
7	
8	t0 = time.time()
9	summary = analyse(BENCH_CONFIG, "access.log", "report.out")
10	ms = (time.time() - t0) * 1000
11	
12	print("CHECKSUM: %d" % summary["checksum"])
13	print("BENCH_MS: %d" % round(ms))
14

file.content:
"""Benchmark: run the full analysis on the bench workload and print one
machine-parseable line. The scorer trusts only this output."""

import time

from logscan import BENCH_CONFIG, analyse

t0 = time.time()
summary = analyse(BENCH_CONFIG, "access.log", "report.out")
ms = (time.time() - t0) * 1000

print("CHECKSUM: %d" % summary["checksum"])
print("BENCH_MS: %d" % round(ms))

content.content:
1	"""Behavioural tests for the log analyser. Any optimisation must keep every
2	assertion green: the report bytes, the aggregates, and the unit-level
3	semantics are all pinned. Run with:  python3 test_logscan.py
4	"""
5	
6	import unittest
7	
8	from logscan import (
9	    TEST_CONFIG,
10	    analyse,
11	    load_lines,
12	    method_weight,
13	    status_class,
14	    top_path_per_hour,
15	)
16	
17	
18	class TestLogscan(unittest.TestCase):
19	    def test_pipeline_aggregates_and_report_bytes(self):
20	        s = analyse(TEST_CONFIG, "test-access.log", "test-report.out")
21	        self.assertEqual(s["entries"], 700)
22	        self.assertEqual(s["flagged"], 3)
23	        self.assertEqual(s["weighted"], 9072)
24	        self.assertEqual(s["total_bytes"], 20954254)
25	        self.assertEqual(s["lines_written"], 20)
26	        self.assertEqual(s["checksum"], 122860189)
27	
28	    def test_status_class_is_stable(self):
29	        self.assertEqual(status_class(404), "4xx-77")
30	        self.assertEqual(status_class(404), "4xx-77")
31	
32	    def test_method_weight_is_stable(self):
33	        self.assertEqual(method_weight("POST", TEST_CONFIG["seed"]), 11)
34	
35	    def test_top_path_breaks_ties_lexically(self):
36	        entries = [
37	            {"path": "/b", "hour": 0},
38	            {"path": "/a", "hour": 0},
39	            {"path": "/b", "hour": 0},
40	            {"path": "/a", "hour": 0},
41	            {"path": "/c", "hour": 1},
42	        ]
43	        tops = top_path_per_hour(entries, 3)
44	        self.assertEqual(tops[0], (0, "/a", 2))
45	        self.assertEqual(tops[1], (1, "/c", 1))
46	        self.assertEqual(tops[2], (2, None, -1))
47	
48	    def test_load_lines_round_trips(self):
49	        with open("test-roundtrip.log", "w") as f:
50	            f.write("alpha beta\ngamma delta\n")
51	        self.assertEqual(load_lines("test-roundtrip.log"), ["alpha beta", "gamma delta"])
52	
53	
54	if __name__ == "__main__":
55	    unittest.main()
56

file.content:
"""Behavioural tests for the log analyser. Any optimisation must keep every
assertion green: the report bytes, the aggregates, and the unit-level
semantics are all pinned. Run with:  python3 test_logscan.py
"""

import unittest

from logscan import (
    TEST_CONFIG,
    analyse,
    load_lines,
    method_weight,
    status_class,
    top_path_per_hour,
)


class TestLogscan(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_pipeline_aggregates_and_report_bytes(self):
        s = analyse(TEST_CONFIG, "test-access.log", "test-report.out")
        self.assertEqual(s["entries"], 700)
        self.assertEqual(s["flagged"], 3)
        self.assertEqual(s["weighted"], 9072)
        self.assertEqual(s["total_bytes"], 20954254)
        self.assertEqual(s["lines_written"], 20)
        self.assertEqual(s["checksum"], 122860189)

    def test_status_class_is_stable(self):
        self.assertEqual(status_class(404), "4xx-77")
        self.assertEqual(status_class(404), "4xx-77")

    def test_method_weight_is_stable(self):
        self.assertEqual(method_weight("POST", TEST_CONFIG["seed"]), 11)

    def test_top_path_breaks_ties_lexically(self):
        entries = [
            {"path": "/b", "hour": 0},
            {"path": "/a", "hour": 0},
            {"path": "/b", "hour": 0},
            {"path": "/a", "hour": 0},
            {"path": "/c", "hour": 1},
        ]
        tops = top_path_per_hour(entries, 3)
        self.assertEqual(tops[0], (0, "/a", 2))
        self.assertEqual(tops[1], (1, "/c", 1))
        self.assertEqual(tops[2], (2, None, -1))

    def test_load_lines_round_trips(self):
        with open("test-roundtrip.log", "w") as f:
            f.write("alpha beta\ngamma delta\n")
        self.assertEqual(load_lines("test-roundtrip.log"), ["alpha beta", "gamma delta"])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

No exported names, signatures, or behaviour changed.

**Result:** BENCH_MS: 3640, CHECKSUM: 2198684379 (matches required baseline).

Done. Tests pass, checksum unchanged at 2198684379, BENCH_MS dropped from 4115 to 524 (~8x faster).

Changes: replaced the O(n²) `top_path_per_hour` nested path-vs-path scan with a single pass building a per-hour path-count dict; replaced the O(n) `host in suspicious` list scan with a set lookup; and added lookup-table caches (`_STATUS_CLASS_CACHE`, `_METHOD_WEIGHT_CACHE`) so `status_class`/`method_weight` derive each distinct status/seed once instead of rescanning their fixed derivation loop for every log entry.

Tests pass and checksum matches (2198684379). Final `BENCH_MS: 1168`.

Changes to `/private/var/folders/t8/bhx1lqx977df536xx99b4f480000gp/T/mdz-agentic-YYtURE/cand5/logscan.py`: hoisted three pieces of loop-invariant work — `status_class` now caches its expensive banding computation per distinct status code instead of redoing the 2000-iteration derivation on every entry; `method_weight` now builds its weight table once per `seed` instead of rebuilding it from scratch on every single call; and `top_path_per_hour` now buckets `entries` by hour once up front instead of rescanning the full entries list for every hour. All exported names/signatures unchanged, behavior identical.

content.text:
**Winner: strategy 1 — reducing algorithmic complexity. BENCH_MS: 520** (from 3905 baseline, ~7.5x).

`logscan.py` now holds that candidate; tests green, checksum `2198684379` unchanged.

| Strategy | Median BENCH_MS |
|---|---|
| 1. algorithmic complexity | **518** |
| 5. hoist loop invariants | 1150 |
| 2. memoisation | 1182 |
| 4. batch I/O | 3455 |
| 3. hash-based sets | 3585 |

Two things worth flagging:

- **The winner overran its brief.** Strategy 1 was scoped to replacing nested scans, but that worker also added caches for `status_class`/`method_weight` and swapped the suspicious-host list for a set — i.e. it absorbed strategies 2, 3 and 5. Its win is partly a wider mandate, not purely a better idea. On like-for-like grounds, hoisting (1150ms) and memoisation (1182ms) are the real single-lever winners, and they're the same lever: the per-entry re-derivation in `status_class` and `method_weight` is where the ~3.9s actually lives.
- **The scoring is noisy by construction.** The map workers benchmarked themselves while four siblings ran concurrently, so their self-reported numbers were inflated and mutually incomparable. I had the reduce step re-measure serially with three runs and a median, which is why cand1 reports 518 here rather than the 524 it claimed. Worth keeping if you run this skill again — the MDZ as written doesn't isolate the timing.

Candidate workspaces are left at `cand1/`–`cand5/` and the original at `base/` if you want to diff or cherry-pick a narrower change.
